comment_text:
I would give it an IGN 7/10. First ill start with the pros:
1. Dante looks good and his humor was basically on par with the games.. mostly.
2. Intro and credits were the best part, music was great.
Now for some cons:
1. Definitely didnt like the humanizing of demon family refugees into the human world. Like wtf, was this written by a pro Palestinian? Honestly if you really stop to think about it the show tries to subtely take a feminist, anti republican/ anti religion, pro terrorist turn. The series has always been united in that all demons are evil, no gray area bs. Dante and Trish are the only exception and that because of a special bond and literally crying blood. Thus, Devil May Cry. Sexualizing Dante and then going full woke and not doing the same for Lady is just stupid. Its okay to do it to males but not females by idiot standards. If they do this to Trish I will just turn it off and not support it. One last good thing, i did like the cameo for certain main character from DMC2.

video_title: Devil May Cry | Mercenary Cleanup | Sneak Peek | Netflix
comment_text:
just binged the show and heres a honest review from a long time dmc fan whose played every game (yes, even the reboot)

For a casual fan, yeah I think its a pretty good show. The animation is pretty top tier (other then the bad cg motorcycle), plot was… all over the place but at least somewhat consistent within the show. its entertaining if you look at it as a reboot and not as any kind of continuation of the mainline series.

Now as a hardcore fan, I have a bone to pick. It really feels like the people at netflix just cherry picked the coolest scenes and characters they could from the main game and smushed it all together with no thought about continuity. I get that this is supposed to be to be its own timeline or alternative universe or something but it just feels lazy. Here are a few main points (warning I will be nitpicking with my extensive knowledge of dmc cannon and lore lol):

1. Lucia showing up at the start getting captured as a demon hunter. Shes definitely suppose to still be on vie de marli as a protector, not gallivanting around as  a demon hunter.

2. Random enemies from all over the timeline showing up?
a.agni and ruda from dmc3, they are suppose to be guarding the temen-ni-gru, wont even be in hell to be recruited by some weird bunny. 
b.echidna from dmc4, the only one that kinda makes sense to be in hell and able to be recruited. 
c. plasma from dmc1. should be stuck on mallet island
d. the worst offender, cavaliere angelo. thats just trish stuffed in an artificial angelo by urizen AKA vergil. seeing as how artificial angelos are created in dmc4 and trish isnt even in the picture yet since dmc1 hasnt happened, none of this makes sense.

3. Trying to redeem arkham in any conceivable way. The show tries to convince you that arkhams just a swell dad who wants to protect his family and went batshit insane. well anyone who played dmc3 will know that arkhams always been batshit insane lol. he married Ladys mom because her blood is needed to unseal the temen-ni-gru, he was never a “good dad”

4. dante and vergils blood being used to unseal the underworld. Once again, should be Ladys blood since her mom is a decedent of the priestess whose blood was used to seal the underworld.

5. vergil somehow already being nelo angelo??? vergil can only be nelo angelo after he falls to the underworld which cant happen if him and dante haven’t had their big fight on the temen-ni-gru which clearly has not happened since dante still things vergil is dead. also, vergil being kinda a free agent and able to transform back into “human form”?? nahhh hes basically a slave of mundus at this point in the timeline, no free will for him.

6. dante having no clue hes half demon. yeah no… hes always known lol, been that way since the dmc novels which happens to be canonically the first in the timeline

7. devil trigger playing for dante. dmc5 fans would know that devil trigger is very much neros theme and not dantes, though with dante being voiced by nero guess that lines already a little blurry.

8. the ending???? wha…what happened??? the ending really threw me completely for a loop like, dante captured and in cryo sleep??? humans setting up shop in the underworld??? there isnt even a vaguest basis of this in the mainline lore… watching it felt like a fever dream. i can only hope the folks at netflix know what they are doing if they plan on bringing this to a season 2 cause thats one weird ass way to end the story.

Now that im done nitpicking, lets talk about some of the more fun stuff. heres a list of some cameos and easter eggs a casual fan might have missed.

1. Goldstein was mentioned making Ladys weapons, and a big crate with KA was foreshadowed. Neil Goldstein was the one who originally made dantes weapons ebony and ivory, and KA stands for Kalina Ann which is ladys signature weapon in game. its named after her mom. also neil is nicos grandmother (not by blood but adoption)

2. The racoon city job. thats the name of the city where resident evil 2 takes place. very cool easter egg.

3. strawberry subdae. nice call back to the original dmc anime where we first see the quaint little dinner and the waitress (cindy) was a minor supporting characters.

4. the pool scene with the mercs. a nice little callback to dmc3 where in the opening dante beats up some demons using billiard balls

5. dantes guns breaking. was mentioned in the books that before dante got ebony and ivory which were custom made for him, he use to break hundguns all the time because they couldnt stand up to his insane trigger finger.

6. Uroboros and arius in the final scene of the last episode. arius is the main enemy from dmc2 and uroboros was his evil corporate company. kinda a weird direction for the show to angle making arius the main villain in the next season given dmc2 was a massive flop but hey, OG arius had god tier voice acting in the stupidest way possible, would be hilarious to see that again

thats all from me now. ill update the lists if I think of anything else. overall, wether youre a hardcore or casual fan or a complete noob to the francise, I would give this show a watch. it was a pretty stylish and fun time even with all the plot holes and inconsistencies lol and if this show brings us any closer to dmc6 then Im here for it.
